creator: c0rn@o2.pl
text:
>You could try editing lisa.sh and remove -q from command_args so lisa
>might print out some diagnostic messages. Perhaps attach your lisarc
>to this PR as well.

Removing q option didn`t turned out any error messages. Here`s my
lisarc:

SecondWait = -1
SearchUsingNmblookup = 1
DeliverUnnamedHosts = 0
FirstWait = 30
MaxPingsAtOnce = 256
UpdatePeriod = 300
PingAddresses = 10.1.0.21/255.255.255.0;
AllowedAddresses = 10.1.0.21/255.255.255.0;
BroadcastNetwork = 10.1.0.21/255.255.255.0;
PingNames =

[corn:~]> ifconfig ndis0
ndis0: flags=8843<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST> mtu 1500
        inet 10.1.0.21 netmask 0xffffff00 broadcast 10.1.0.255
        ether 00:14:a5:a5:3a:0d
        media: IEEE 802.11 Wireless Ethernet autoselect (OFDM/54Mbps)
        status: associated
        ssid slowicza.org channel 7 bssid 00:0e:2e:7d:1a:14
        authmode OPEN privacy OFF txpowmax 100 bmiss 7 protmode CTS
